results.question: Does the 'Install Tauri CLI' step exist and use compatible version?

evidence.findings: Line 44-45: '- name: Install Tauri CLI' with 'run: cargo install tauri-cli --version "^2"'

results.examiner_notes: Step exists before Build release at line 44 with correct Tauri v2 version constraint

results.question: Does Build release use cargo tauri build?

evidence.findings: Line 50: 'cargo tauri build --target ${{ matrix.target }}' - no 'cd src-tauri' or '--release' flag

results.examiner_notes: Build release runs from project root using cargo tauri build as required

results.question: Does Package macOS step include DMG handling?

evidence.findings: Lines 60-63: cd ../dmg, DMG_FILE=$(ls *.dmg), shasum -a 256 checksum, mv with 6-level path ../../../../../..

results.examiner_notes: DMG handling correctly adds to Package macOS step with checksum and proper relative path

results.question: Does Upload step include *.dmg and *.dmg.sha256?
results.examiner_notes: Upload step includes both *.dmg and *.dmg.sha256 alongside existing tar.gz entries

results.question: Does release body reference .dmg with Textbrush naming and drag-to-Applications?

evidence.findings: Lines 85-90: References 'Textbrush_x.y.z_aarch64.dmg' and 'Textbrush_x.y.z_x64.dmg', includes 'drag Textbrush to Applications' and 'xattr -cr' Gatekeeper bypass, plus 'Alternative: Download the .tar.gz' note

results.examiner_notes: Release body correctly updated with DMG instructions as primary macOS distribution format

results.question: Is Linux packaging step unchanged?

evidence.findings: git diff shows Package Linux step is completely unchanged from original: same tar -czf, sha256sum, mv commands. Only additions are in macOS section and upload files list.

results.examiner_notes: Linux packaging is identical to original per git diff

results.question: Are all 5 required modifications present?

evidence.findings: 1) Install Tauri CLI step at line 44, 2) cargo tauri build at line 50, 3) DMG handling in Package macOS at lines 60-63, 4) *.dmg in Upload files at lines 79-80, 5) .dmg in release body at line 85

results.examiner_notes: All 5 required changes are present and correct

results.question: Are mv path depths correct?

evidence.findings: macOS tarball: mv ... ../../../../../.. (6 levels, correct from 6-deep bundle/macos). DMG: mv ... ../../../../../.. (6 levels, correct from 6-deep bundle/dmg). Linux: mv ... ../../../../.. (5 levels, unchanged from original).

results.examiner_notes: All mv paths are correct per spec requirements

test_gate.command: python3.12 -m pytest tests/ -m 'not slow and not integration' (torch-dependent tests excluded)
failures: 24 pre-existing torch-dependency failures (confirmed pre-existing on master, unrelated to this YAML change)
test_gate.evidence: 649 passing, 24 pre-existing failures (identical to baseline). No regressions introduced. This is a YAML-only workflow change with no application code modifications.

e2e_tests.command: N/A - GitHub Actions workflow changes cannot be executed locally
e2e_tests.evidence: This story modifies only .github/workflows/release.yml - a CI/CD workflow executed on GitHub Actions infrastructure. There is no local E2E test infrastructure applicable to this type of change. All acceptance criteria are verified through static analysis of the YAML structure. The acceptance-criteria.md explicitly documents all criteria as 'Type: manual'. All 6 acceptance criteria verified by YAML inspection: AC-001 through AC-006 all pass.
